items.title: Document and stress-test your prior
items.avoid: Picking hyperparameters because they 'feel reasonable' without recording rationale.
items.prefer: State the prior in plain language, simulate prior predictive data, and vary hyperparameters ±2×.
items.why: Stakeholders can challenge assumptions; you catch priors that dominate unintentionally.
items.example: Before A/B analysis, plot Beta(2,8) vs Beta(5,5) priors on conversion rate and show resulting 95% credible intervals side by side.

items.title: Match prior strength to data volume
items.avoid: Using a very tight prior when you only have dozens of observations.
items.prefer: Weaken the prior (or use hierarchical partial pooling) until the likelihood provides enough signal.
items.why: Overconfident priors freeze beliefs and understate uncertainty when data are sparse.
items.example: With 5 clinical cases, prefer Beta(1,1) or empirically informed Beta(α,β) from literature instead of Beta(1000,1000).
